title: Midnight Craving Charcoal-Tender British Fish Tray
description: A charred, tender, midnight-craving British dish featuring rum, bonito shave, and a shojin-friendly banana leaf vessel.

ingredients.name: Atlantic Cod Fillets
ingredients.notes: Skin removed, cut into thick chunk

ingredients.name: Banana Leaves
ingredients.notes: Fresh, large enough to wrap each piece

ingredients.name: Potato
ingredients.notes: High starch, peeled, cut into chunk

instructions: Soak the potato chunks in vinegar for at least 30 minutes to remove excess starch.
Drain and pat dry the potato chunks with paper towels.
Cut the cod fillets into large chunks and season with salt, pepper, and a pinch of brown sugar.
Dredge the cod chunks in flour, shaking off excess.
Dip the floured cod chunks in the beaten eggs and then coat in panko breadcrumbs.
In a large bowl, whisk together the rum, lemon juice, and softened butter until well combined.
Add the cod chunks to the marinade and mix to coat.
Heat a large charcoal grill to medium-high heat.
Wrap each piece of cod in a banana leaf and place on the grill.
Cook for 8-10 minutes per side, or until the cod is cooked through.
Transfer the cooked cod to a serving tray and top with a sprinkle of bonito flakes.
Serve immediately with a side of charred potato wedges.
